blurb:
Josie has floppy ears, a nubby tail, and a personality that her foster describes as a 7-8 out of 10 on the energy scale, matched by an equally high cuddle level. That's a fun combination if you're the right person for it, and she's actively looking for that person right now.

She's a 6-year-old hound mix, about 35 pounds, and she came back to Mostly Mutts after a short adoption because her energy wasn't a match for that family. No drama, just an honest mismatch. She went through the prison foster training program, learned a lot, and is currently doing well in a foster home. She's playful, affectionate, and loves spending time with people. She does some mouthy play but her foster says it's never mean, just enthusiastic. Older kids, maybe 10 and up, would probably get along great with her.

She needs to be your only dog and would do best without cats. If you've got an active lifestyle and you've been looking for a dog who will actually keep up with you on a trail or a long walk and then crash on the couch with you afterward, Josie is describing herself.

Mostly Mutts is based in Kennesaw. Head to their website to submit an inquiry and get a meet on the calendar.
scoring_notes:
• Prison training program plus a returned adoption gives Josie two layered story angles that together create real editorial texture
• Her only-dog requirement and energy level make her a strong fit for a specific active-lifestyle reader segment, which sharpens the call to action
• A second time through rescue at 6 years old with documented placement history suggests she has been cycling through care longer than her profile implies

blurb:
Jacquelyn came into rescue from animal control and landed in a foster home where she has apparently been thriving ever since. She's about 2 years old, 58 pounds, and by all accounts a wiggle-your-way-into-your-lap kind of dog. Her foster describes her as lovable, huggable, and sweet, and says she brings a lot of playful, silly energy to the house without being overwhelming about it.

She's a Velcro dog through and through. She wants to be near you, she wants to be part of whatever you're doing, and she's learned to dial it back when asked. She knows "sit," "no," and "back," and she's been great overnight without any potty breaks. She does need a few trips outside during the day, so a work-from-home or stay-at-home situation is the ideal setup for her.

Here's the thing worth knowing: Jacquelyn has a congenital kidney defect. It's manageable and hasn't slowed her down at all, but it does mean her lifespan may be a little shorter than average. Her foster is upfront about it, and so are we. If you're someone who can give a happy, high-quality life to a dog who will love you completely for however long she's here, Jacquelyn might be exactly who you're looking for. She does well with other dogs and would probably do best without cats. Kids are okay depending on age, though toddlers might get bowled over by her enthusiasm.

Mostly Mutts is a rescue based in Kennesaw. You can reach out through their website to start the process and arrange a meet.
scoring_notes:
• The congenital kidney defect is a rare, deeply human story about choosing love over longevity that will stop readers mid-scroll
• Her affectionate Velcro personality and basic obedience make her genuinely appealing despite the health caveat
• Work-from-home requirement and medical complexity suggest she has been harder to place, implying a longer wait than typical fosters

blurb:
Yasmeen spent time in a prison foster and training program before landing in her current foster home, and her foster mom says whoever adopts her is going to hit the jackpot. That's a strong endorsement, and from everything in her profile, it tracks. She's 8 years old, about 58 pounds, and the kind of dog who becomes deeply loyal once she decides she trusts you.

She was a little uncertain around new people when she first came in. That's not a red flag, it's just who she is. She needs to size you up before she opens up, and once she does, she's all in. In the training program, that trust-building took about a day. She's good with other dogs, good with cats, and has met kids as young as 7 without any issues, though the rescue recommends dog-savvy kids 15 and up given that she does some guarding in her home space. The fix for that is simple: introduce new people to her outside first, on neutral ground, before bringing them into the house. That one adjustment makes a real difference.

For the right person, Yasmeen is a dream. She wants a calm home, a consistent routine, and someone who will let her be loyal to them. If you're not in a rush and you appreciate a dog who takes her relationships seriously, she's worth meeting.

Reach out through Mostly Mutts to set up an introduction. They'll want to do a proper meet, especially if you have other pets.
scoring_notes:
• Prison training program backstory is one of the most compelling rescue narratives available and will generate genuine reader curiosity
• At 8 years old with guarding tendencies and specific household requirements, her adopter profile is narrow, signaling a longer road to placement
• The jackpot endorsement from her foster paired with her loyalty-first personality is a powerful editorial hook for a senior dog feature

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Beluga came into Mostly Mutts from animal control with her siblings, and at about two months old and 1.5 pounds, she is very much at the beginning of everything. She's got the full kitten package: chases anything that moves, wriggles, rings, or flies, warms up quickly once she feels comfortable, and then turns into a cuddly little shadow once she decides you're her person.

She's already litter trained and microchipped. She'll need her kitten shots finished as she grows, and her spay is required but covered by Mostly Mutts through one of their approved vets in the North Metro Atlanta area. That's also why this adoption is local only, so keep that in mind.

Beluga gets along with her siblings, so another cat at home would likely be a good fit. Dogs are unknown. Gentle kids are welcome. She just needs a little time to warm up at first, which is pretty reasonable for a kitten who's new to everything.

Her adoption fee is $175 and she's in Kennesaw. Contact Mostly Mutts to come meet her before she's gone.
scoring_notes:
• Classic kitten appeal with covered spay costs and microchip already done lowers the barrier to adoption significantly
• Origin from animal control with siblings gives just enough rescue narrative without a heavy backstory
• At roughly two months old and newly arrived, she has almost certainly been in care only a short time

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
She came in as part of a litter, got adopted, and then life threw her a curveball. Gina is about 11 months old, roughly 5 pounds, and she has been back at Mostly Mutts after a return adoption tied to some ongoing stomach issues. Nobody knows exactly why she gets sick sometimes. The tests haven't given clear answers. But her foster mom calls her the best cat ever, and that's not nothing.

Gina is independent but also loves to snuggle, which is honestly the ideal combination. She's playful, she's gentle, and because she stayed small, she's still got that kitten energy at almost a year old. If you've ever wanted a petite cat who never quite grew out of her baby phase, Gina is basically that.

The one thing to know: she's on a special diet, so if you have other cats at home, you'd need a plan for feeding her separately. That's manageable for the right person, and it's worth it. She's done fine with the adult cats in her foster home and even stayed calm around a dog on a leash. Her foster says gentle kids are welcome too. She just needs someone willing to work with her a little.

Gina is located in Kennesaw and available through Mostly Mutts. Submit an inquiry through their website or reach out directly to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Returned adoption with unresolved health mystery adds emotional weight that resonates with compassionate readers
• Special diet requirement narrows the pool but the right adopter will find her irresistible at under a pound and kitten-energy at 11 months
• Second stint at the shelter after a failed adoption signals she has been waiting longer than her age suggests

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
He was adopted from Mostly Mutts as a kitten, grew up in a family that loved him, and then circumstances changed and he had to come back. That's the whole story. No behavior issues, no drama. Just a 5-year-old cat who got shuffled around through no fault of his own and is now ready to settle somewhere for good.

Iceman is 11 pounds, fully vaccinated, neutered, microchipped, and combo tested negative. He likes hanging out with his people and watching the world go by. His previous foster had four kids and he was great with all of them. He's litter trained, he keeps his space clean, and by all accounts he's as easy as cats get.

The one compatibility note: he wasn't a fan of other male cats in his previous home. A female cat housemate would likely be fine, and dogs are unknown since he hasn't been tested. For the right household, none of that is a dealbreaker. He just wants to be somewhere that's his again.

Iceman is in Kennesaw and his adoption fee is $175. Reach out to Mostly Mutts by phone or email to set up a meeting.
scoring_notes:
• A fully vetted, family-tested adult cat with zero behavior issues is the easiest possible adoption for a busy household
• The returned-through-no-fault-of-his-own narrative is instantly sympathetic and drives emotional urgency for readers
• At 5 years old and back in rescue after a full upbringing elsewhere, Iceman has clearly been waiting for a second chapter longer than most

blurb:
Jacquelyn came into rescue from animal control and landed in a foster home where she has been thriving ever since. She's about two years old, weighs around 58 pounds, and has the kind of personality that fills a room. Her foster describes her as lovable, huggable, and genuinely silly, the kind of dog who wiggles her way into your lap and makes it very hard to say no.

She's a Velcro dog through and through. She wants to be near you, knows her commands, and is working on being polite about demanding attention (she's getting there). She also takes her role as household alert system very seriously. Nothing gets past Jacquelyn, and she will absolutely let you know about it.

One thing worth knowing: Jacquelyn has a congenital kidney condition that's easily managed and hasn't slowed her down at all. She does best with a family where someone is home during the day, since she needs a few bathroom breaks, but her foster says it's no more than any other dog. She'd love a home with another friendly dog and does best without cats. Because of her enthusiasm, a home without toddlers would be a safer fit. She has a shorter expected lifespan than most dogs, but she is absolutely living her best life right now and looking for someone to live it with.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Check their website to start an inquiry and set up a meet.
scoring_notes:
• Congenital kidney condition and shortened lifespan create genuine urgency — this is a now-or-never adoption story
• Lovable, silly Velcro personality paired with a health challenge is a high-emotion combination built for newsletter impact
• Narrower household requirements reduce the adopter pool, making targeted visibility through a newsletter critical to placement

blurb:
Josie is a six-year-old hound mix with floppy ears, a nubby little tail, and enough energy to make you actually use that gym membership. She came up through the Mostly Mutts prison training program, got adopted for a couple of months, and came back when the energy level wasn't a match. That's not a knock on Josie. That's just an honest dog in the wrong home.

Her foster puts her energy at a seven or eight out of ten, but here's the thing: her cuddle level matches it. She wants to run, play, and go on adventures, and then she wants to collapse on the couch with you at the end of the day. If you're someone who actually gets outside regularly and has been looking for a reason to do more of it, Josie is a very good reason.

She does some mouthy play, never aggressive, just enthusiastic, so older kids around ten and up would be a better fit. She'd prefer to be your only dog for now, and cats aren't her thing either. But if you've got an active household without other pets and you're ready for a dog who will genuinely keep up with you, she is worth the drive to Kennesaw.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit their website to submit an inquiry and arrange a meet with Josie.
scoring_notes:
• Prison training program graduate who was adopted and returned gives her a layered, compelling story with clear second-chance appeal
• Adventure-dog-plus-couch-dog duality speaks directly to active readers looking for a lifestyle companion
• Return after adoption suggests she has been cycling through the system, adding quiet urgency that rewards the right active-household adopter

blurb:
Yasmeen is eight years old, about 58 pounds, and her foster mom says whoever adopts her is going to hit the jackpot. That is a direct quote and, based on everything else in her profile, it tracks.

She came to Mostly Mutts from animal control and went through their prison training program, where she learned a lot and showed just how quickly she bonds with people she trusts. In her foster home she's been gentle, loving, and good with both dogs and cats. She does like to keep an eye on who's coming and going, and she's protective of the people she loves, but that protectiveness has settled down a lot since she's been in a stable home. The recommendation is to introduce her to new people outside the house first, which is a pretty small ask for a dog this loyal.

She's not the right fit for young kids, but for a quieter household or someone who wants a devoted, calm companion who's already done the chaotic puppy years, she's exactly that. She knows who she loves and she shows up for them completely.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Check their website to start an application and set up a time to meet Yasmeen with one of their trainers.
scoring_notes:
• Foster's 'jackpot' endorsement combined with prison program graduation is a rare dual credibility signal that commands attention
• Eight-year-old senior from animal control who thrives with both dogs and cats is an unusually strong compatibility profile for her age bracket
• Senior dogs from animal control statistically wait longest — her age and origin make visibility in a newsletter directly impactful to her outcome

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Beluga came in from animal control with her siblings, and at about 1.5 lbs and two months old, she is still very much in the tiny-and-chaotic phase of kittenhood. She is litter trained, already microchipped, and absolutely going to chase any toy that wiggles, flies, or makes noise. That part is guaranteed.

She needs a few minutes to warm up when she meets someone new, which is completely fair given that everything is still new to her. Once she decides you're okay, the rescue says she becomes a content and happy companion. She has grown up around her siblings, so she would likely enjoy having another cat at home to keep her company. A resident cat who has a little patience for kitten energy would probably be a good match.

One logistical note: because she is still small, her spay surgery hasn't happened yet. Mostly Mutts covers that cost through one of their approved vets in the North Metro Atlanta area, so this one is local adoptions only. You'll just need to follow through on the remaining kitten vaccine schedule as she grows.

Beluga is in Kennesaw with Mostly Mutts. Adoption fee is $175. Reach out by phone or email to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Tiny, playful, two-month-old kitten is high-demand inventory — strong immediate appeal but low urgency
• Local-only restriction due to pending spay limits reach but creates a clear, actionable call to action for nearby readers
• Backstory is straightforward shelter intake with no standout narrative hook to drive emotional engagement

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Iceman was adopted from Mostly Mutts as a kitten, spent five years with one family, and came back when their situation changed. None of that is his fault, and he is clearly not holding any grudges. He is 11 lbs, fully neutered, up to date on vaccines, microchipped, and combo tested negative. He came back healthy and ready to do this again.

He is a hang-out cat. He likes being around his people and watching the world go by from a comfortable spot. Not high-maintenance, not demanding, just present and companionable. His foster profile is still building, but his previous family had him for five years, which says something. Cats don't stick around for that long in a home that isn't working for them.

A note on other pets: he reportedly was not a fan of other male cats in his previous home, but did well around kids. If you have a female cat already, there's a good chance they'd get along. Dogs are unknown, so a meet and greet would be the right call if that's your situation.

Iceman is with Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Adoption fee is $175. Email or call to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Five-year track record in a loving home is a built-in credibility signal that reassures cautious adopters
• Returned through no fault of his own is a classic second-chance narrative that resonates with newsletter readers
• Moderate pet compatibility limits widen the adopter pool enough to make this a practical, promotable candidate

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Her foster mom calls her the best cat ever, and that is a direct quote. Gina is an 11-month-old domestic shorthair who came in as part of the Go-Gos litter, got adopted, and was returned after she started having some stomach issues. Several months of vet care and food changes later, she is doing much better, though she does still vomit occasionally and the vets haven't pinned down an exact cause. That part is worth knowing upfront. What's also worth knowing: she is playful, sweet, pretty independent, and loves to snuggle when she feels like it.

At about 5 lbs, she is tiny and will probably stay that way. If you've always wanted a cat that keeps a little of that kitten energy and kitten size into adulthood, Gina fits that description. She gets along well with other cats and has been fine around dogs and kids too, as long as everyone is calm and gentle with her.

Because she's on a special diet, the rescue asks that any multi-cat household have a plan for feeding her separately. It's a small logistical thing and totally manageable. She just needs someone who will advocate for her a little and give her the routine she does well with.

Gina is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Reach out by phone or email to set up a time to meet her. Adoption fee is $175.
scoring_notes:
• Returned after adoption and treated for months signals a meaningful wait — urgency is real
• Foster's 'best cat ever' endorsement plus tiny kitten-forever size is a strong emotional hook for readers
• Special diet caveat is manageable but narrows the pool, making the right adopter match more valuable to highlight
